name: Tensorpage
one_liner: Paged KV-cache allocator for long-context inference on commodity GPUs.
discovery_note: Found by the scanner, not by an introduction. First observed 2023-09-14, seven months before any funding announcement would have surfaced them.

founders.name: Marisol Ferreira
founders.resolution_note: GitHub mferreira-tp and HN mferreira matched on a shared release URL.

evidence.span: commit 4b91e0c "pagekv: block table with refcounted physical pages"
"You're right that 16 is arbitrary. It's a config knob in main as of an hour ago; defaults are a benchmark artifact, not a belief."
"Our v0.1 numbers overstated the win by ~18% because we benchmarked against an unbatched baseline. Corrected table below."
evidence.note: Self-correction against their own headline number. Weighted heavily.

evidence.span: v0.5.0 — "disaggregated prefill/decode with RDMA page migration. 148 dependent repos as of tagging."
v0.4.0 — "per-tenant quotas, fail-closed. Two production users on this path."
"We report a 2.3x increase in achievable batch size at 128k context with 1.7% throughput overhead."

gate.rationale: Eighteen months of iteration on a single artifact, with a public self-correction against their own benchmark. Band tightens monotonically as evidence accumulates.

claims.claim_text: 2.3x increase in achievable batch size at 128k context, 1.7% throughput overhead
claims.corroborating_span: v0.3.0 — "benchmark harness now reproducible from a single make target"
dependent graph, counted at tag time

trace_note: The moving score line is the same events replayed at six as_of dates. No number here was computed with knowledge of a later event.
